findings.evidence: `HopfBaseChange.lean:41-42` imposes `[Semiring A] [_root_.HopfAlgebra R A]`, while the body at `:75-76` is `Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight`; Mathlib declares `includeRight : B →ₐ[R] A ⊗[R] B` at `Mathlib/RingTheory/TensorProduct/Basic.lean:323` under only `[Semiring A] [Algebra R A] [Semiring B] [Algebra R B]` from `:248-251`.
findings.fix: Move `BaseChange`, `baseChangeIncludeRight`, and `baseChangeIncludeRight_apply` to an algebra-level section with `[Semiring A] [Algebra R A]`; keep the counit/antipode/comul lemmas in the Hopf section.
findings.issue: `baseChangeIncludeRight` is an ordinary tensor-product algebra map but is declared under the section assumption `[_root_.HopfAlgebra R A]`, so users need a Hopf structure just to use the canonical inclusion.

findings.evidence: `HopfBaseChange.lean:107` requires `[CommSemiring B]`, but Mathlib’s `AlgHom.liftEquiv` has `[Semiring B]` at `Mathlib/RingTheory/TensorProduct/Basic.lean:762-764` and the same equivalence at `:774`.
findings.fix: Generalize the target variables to `[Semiring B] [Algebra S B] [Algebra R B] [IsScalarTower R S B]` and similarly `[Semiring C] ...`; only add commutativity in separate point-functor specializations if needed.
findings.issue: `baseChangeValue` and its target-naturality lemmas require `[CommSemiring B]` (and later `[CommSemiring C]`) even though the adjunction being packaged is valid for noncommutative target semirings.

summary: The added API is mostly at the intended base-change level, but two declarations inherit stronger algebraic hypotheses than Mathlib’s corresponding tensor-product API requires.
